whatItDoes: Formats, validates, and minifies YAML content with configurable indentation, key sorting, and syntax validation. Detects YAML-to-JSON conversion for programmatic use, supports multi-document YAML streams, and provides detailed error messages for malformed YAML.
whyItExists: YAML is widely used for configuration files (Docker Compose, Kubernetes, CI/CD pipelines) but is notoriously sensitive to indentation errors. This tool helps developers validate, format, and convert YAML without hunting for invisible whitespace bugs.
whoShouldUse: DevOps engineers managing Kubernetes or Docker Compose configs, CI/CD pipeline maintainers, Python developers using YAML for configuration, and anyone debugging YAML formatting issues.
useCases: Format a poorly indented docker-compose.yml file to make it readable and consistent
Validate YAML syntax before deploying to production to catch indentation bugs early
Convert YAML to JSON for programmatic consumption in JavaScript/Python scripts
Minify YAML by removing comments and whitespace for smaller file sizes in deployment
Sort keys alphabetically to enforce consistent key ordering across team config files
instructions: Paste or upload your YAML content into the input area
Set indent size (2 or 4 spaces) — YAML does not support tabs for indentation
Toggle validation: the tool checks for common YAML errors (mixed indentation, trailing spaces, duplicate keys)
Select output format: Formatted YAML, Minified YAML, or JSON
Enable key sorting to alphabetically order top-level keys
Click "Format" and review the output; copy or download the result
examples: Input: 'services:  web:    image: nginx' with 2-space indent. Output: 'services:\n  web:\n    image: nginx'
bestPractices: Always validate YAML before committing to version control — indentation bugs are hard to spot visually
Use 2-space indentation consistently, the most common standard across YAML projects
Run YAML through this tool after merging PRs to catch formatting regressions
Convert to JSON when debugging YAML parsing issues — JSON's strict syntax makes errors more obvious
commonMistakes: Using tabs instead of spaces for indentation, which causes a YAML parsing error
Inconsistent indentation depth within the same file (mixing 2-space and 4-space blocks)
Forgetting to quote strings with special characters like colons, hashes, or brackets
Assuming booleans are always lowercase; YAML accepts multiple boolean formats (yes/no, true/false, on/off)
faq: What YAML version does this support? YAML 1.2 with most common 1.1 features.
Can I convert YAML to JSON? Yes, the tool supports YAML-to-JSON conversion as an output option.
Does it handle multi-document YAML (--- separator)? Yes, each document is processed independently.
What about anchors and aliases? Anchors (&) and aliases (*) are preserved in formatted output but resolved in JSON conversion.
